search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae neck pain telehealth guideline PDF
site:doh.gov.ae neck pain guideline Abu Dhabi
rejection_reasons: The current exact DHA guideline was available.
Patient pages and summaries were not treated as exact clinician guidance.
Treatment details were not required for evidence mapping.
population_applicability: Partial: adult neck pain only; paediatric, major-trauma, and diagnosis-specific presentations require separate pathways.
setting_applicability: Partial: Dubai telehealth source versus setting-agnostic baseline with in-person examination prompts.
UAE_applicability: Verified for DHA-licensed telehealth in Dubai, United Arab Emirates; broader UAE application is not asserted.
recency_verification: Official Issue 2 copy dated 2024-02-21, effective 2024-04-21, revision due 2029-02-21, opened on 2026-07-12.
superseded_check: Issue 2 is current on the DHA telehealth guidance portal.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped neck-pain concern and specific-negative documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires red-flag identification and lists myelopathic symptoms, fever, immunosuppression, weight loss, cancer, systemic symptoms, chest symptoms, and significant trauma.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped onset, duration, radiation, paraesthesia, function, posture, range, palpation, gait, and neurological examination fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports onset, duration, pain character, arm radiation, paraesthesia, activity impact, posture, movement, range of motion, palpation, neurological assessment, gait, and provocative-manoeuvre documentation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped radiography and MRI review only in selected concern or persistent-symptom contexts.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: States that atraumatic neck pain without red flags generally needs no imaging and reserves radiography, CT, or MRI for red flags, progressive neurology, or persistent moderate-to-severe pain.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped trauma, neurological, fever, weight-loss, worsening, and clinician-arranged review items.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists trauma, persistent symptoms, headache, numbness or tingling, lower-limb weakness, gait or coordination issues, bowel or bladder dysfunction, immunosuppression, weight loss, fever, and chest symptoms.

unresolved_source_gaps: The legacy cervical-strain label, torticollis aliases, shoulder-abduction test, analgesia, ergonomic advice, and fixed follow-up periods remain unsupported by this mapping.
No diagnosis or normal neurological finding may be inferred.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
